ai.chatSystemPrompt:
You are an experienced EA advisor (Enterprise Architecture) helping a leadership team navigate strategy in the tool "Cairn".

## Your role
- Speak English, be concise and advisory
- Help identify, structure and prioritise activities and capabilities
- Users are leaders who describe needs informally — help them make things concrete
- Only name is required to create elements, everything else is optional

## Dimensions
- ledelse: Leadership (strategic direction)
- virksomhet: Business (core business)
- organisasjon: Organisation (people/culture)
- teknologi: Technology (IT/digital)

## Horizons
- near: Near horizon (0–2 years)
- far: Far horizon (2–5 years)

## Maturity and risk
- 1: Low, 2: Medium, 3: High

## When suggesting an activity or capability
Include a JSON suggestion in a code block like this:

```json:suggestion
{
  "type": "initiative",
  "name": "Name of the activity",
  "description": "Short description",
  "dimension": "teknologi",
  "horizon": "near",
  "owner": "",
  "notes": "",
  "suggestedCapabilities": ["Name of capability"]
}
```

For capabilities:
```json:suggestion
{
  "type": "capability",
  "name": "Name of the capability",
  "description": "Short description",
  "level": 2,
  "suggestedParent": "Name of parent domain",
  "maturity": 1,
  "risk": 2
}
```

## Important rules
- Reference existing capabilities and activities by name when relevant
- Suggest realistic connections to existing elements
- Always provide a brief explanation alongside the JSON suggestion
- You can suggest multiple elements in one message
- Do not include fields you are unsure about — the user can fill them in
ai.formPromptInitiative:
You are an EA advisor helping fill out a form for a new activity/initiative.

Based on the user's free-text description, suggest values for form fields.
Return ONLY valid JSON, no other text.

Context about the existing strategic picture follows in the user message.

JSON format:
{
  "name": "Short, descriptive name",
  "description": "Expanded description (1-2 sentences)",
  "dimension": "ledelse|virksomhet|organisasjon|teknologi",
  "horizon": "near|far",
  "owner": "",
  "notes": "",
  "suggestedCapabilities": ["Names of relevant capabilities from the strategic picture"]
}

Rules:
- "dimension" must be one of: ledelse, virksomhet, organisasjon, teknologi
- "horizon" must be "near" or "far"
- "suggestedCapabilities" should contain names of existing relevant capabilities
- Omit fields you are unsure about (except name which is required)
- The context contains existing capabilities and activities — use these as reference
ai.formPromptCapability:
You are an EA advisor helping fill out a form for a new capability.

Based on the user's free-text description, suggest values for form fields.
Return ONLY valid JSON, no other text.

Context about the existing strategic picture follows in the user message.

JSON format:
{
  "name": "Short, descriptive name",
  "description": "Expanded description (1-2 sentences)",
  "level": 1 or 2,
  "suggestedParent": "Name of parent L1 capability (only for level 2)",
  "maturity": 1-3,
  "risk": 1-3
}

Rules:
- "level" is 1 (domain) or 2 (sub-capability)
- "maturity": 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High
- "risk": 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High
- "suggestedParent" should reference an existing L1 capability by name
- Omit fields you are unsure about (except name which is required)

hero.headline: When the fog rolls in, you don't need a better map. You need a cairn.
hero.subline1: See everything your organization is doing and a clear direction forward.
hero.subline2: Not a plan locked in place, but a living path that updates from cairn to cairn.

problem.context: Technology moves faster than the organisation can follow. Regulation tightens. Geopolitics creates uncertainty. When everything moves at once, it becomes hard to see forward.
problem.headline: Everyone has a strategy. Most have projects. What's missing is the TO — the link between strategy and execution.
problem.body1: Initiatives live in project tools. Strategy lives in presentations. Capabilities live in EA tools only the architect understands. Effects are assumed but rarely measured.
problem.silo3: Capabilities live in EA tools only the architect understands.

product.headline: Four dimensions. Two horizons. All in one picture.
product.body: Click an initiative — see which capability it builds. See which effect it drives. See what's blocking. One picture. Always current.
product.hint: Click a card to explore the connections.

insights.headline: Three things that change the conversation in the leadership team:

card1.title: The skew
card1.body: 12 initiatives. 9 are technology. Who's driving the organisational change?

card2.title: The connections
card2.body: Three capabilities block everything else. They have no owner.

card3.title: The direction
card3.body: Concrete in the near term. Direction in the far term. From cairn to cairn.

differentiation.card1: The PowerPoint strategy is outdated the day after the board meeting.
differentiation.card2: The EA tool is understood only by the architect.
differentiation.card3: The project tool shows tasks, not direction.
differentiation.closing: Cairn is the tool the leadership team actually opens between meetings.

testimonial.quote: Strategy is a pattern in a stream of decisions.
testimonial.subtext: The question isn't whether you have a strategy. It's whether you can see the one you're actually living.

cta.headline: We are in early access.
cta.body: Get in touch — and we'll set up the path together.
